description:
You are playing the role of a build-/release-engineer who must prepare a compressed package of build artifacts, verify its integrity and stage it for the QA team. Everything takes place in the normal user’s home directory (/home/user); no privileged access is required. 1. Source material • Create a directory called /home/user/build_artifacts. • Inside that directory create exactly three regular files with the following *exact* names and textual contents (each line below ends with a single UNIX line-feed \n): 1. libfoo-1.0.so 
dummy binary: foo version 1.0\n
2. libbar-2.1.so 
dummy binary: bar version 2.1\n
3. README.txt 
Build Artifacts for sprint 23\n
2. Create a compressed archive • Working from /home/user, produce a gzip-compressed tarball called build_2023-10-31.tar.gz. • The tar archive *must* preserve the path build_artifacts/… inside itself (i.e. when someone lists the archive, they should see build_artifacts/libfoo-1.0.so, etc.). 3. Stage the release candidate • Create a directory /home/user/release_candidate. • Extract the contents of build_2023-10-31.tar.gz into that directory so the resulting on-disk layout is /home/user/release_candidate/build_artifacts/… containing the three files created in step 1. 4. Produce an integrity manifest • Still inside /home/user/release_candidate, generate a file named manifest.sha256. • The manifest must contain *exactly* three lines, one for each file found inside build_artifacts/, in the canonical output format of the sha256sum command: build_artifacts/FILENAME Example of one correctly-formatted line (digest shown here is illustrative; yours will differ): e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 build_artifacts/libfoo-1.0.so • There must be two consecutive spaces between the 64-character hex digest and the relative path, and there must be a trailing newline at the end of the file. 5. Verification log • Create a log file at /home/user/release_candidate/verification.log. • Append (not overwrite) one line for each of the following events, in this exact sequence, each line ending with \n: 1. “ARCHIVE_CREATED” 2. “ARCHIVE_EXTRACTED” 3. “MANIFEST_GENERATED” • No timestamps or extra text—just the keywords in the order above, one per line. What the automated grader will check • All paths exist exactly as specified. • The three source files have the precise byte contents listed. • build_2023-10-31.tar.gz contains only the directory build_artifacts and the three files inside it. • After extraction, the staged files inside /home/user/release_candidate/build_artifacts are byte-for-byte identical to the originals. • Every line in manifest.sha256 matches the regular expression ^[0-9a-f]{64} build_artifacts\/(libfoo-1\.0\.so|libbar-2\.1\.so|README\.txt)$ and the digest values are correct for the *current* staged files. • verification.log exists and contains exactly three lines with the required keywords in the prescribed order. Use whatever standard GNU/Linux CLI utilities you prefer (e.g., mkdir, echo, tar, sha256sum, etc.). The entire task can be accomplished in well under ten shell commands.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth reference the grader will use.

        1. Files and their exact byte contents (including the trailing \n):
    -->
    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/build_artifacts/libfoo-1.0.so">dummy binary: foo version 1.0\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/build_artifacts/libbar-2.1.so">dummy binary: bar version 2.1\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/build_artifacts/README.txt">Build Artifacts for sprint 23\n</file>
    </files>

    <!-- 2. Expected archive -->
    <archive>/home/user/build_2023-10-31.tar.gz</archive>

    <!-- 3. Expected extraction directory -->
    <extractionDir>/home/user/release_candidate</extractionDir>

    <!-- 4. manifest.sha256 must contain exactly these three relative paths in ANY order;
            grader will recompute digests on the fly and compare. -->
    <manifestRelativePaths>
        build_artifacts/libfoo-1.0.so
        build_artifacts/libbar-2.1.so
        build_artifacts/README.txt
    </manifestRelativePaths>

    <!-- 5. verification.log required content -->
    <verificationLogLines>
        ARCHIVE_CREATED
        ARCHIVE_EXTRACTED
        MANIFEST_GENERATED
    </verificationLogLines>
name: task_000000_440dead5
